name: Woodland Bank
description_short: Woodland Bank is a regional FDIC-insured bank serving Minnesota with personal and business checking, savings, loans, and credit card products.
description_long:
Woodland Bank operates as a community-focused financial institution headquartered in Minnesota, serving customers across multiple locations including Deer River, Hill City, Grand Rapids, and Cohasset. The bank is FDIC-insured, meaning deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government up to applicable limits. The institution maintains a traditional banking model while incorporating modern digital conveniences.

Woodland Bank offers a comprehensive suite of deposit and lending products for both personal and business customers. On the deposit side, they provide multiple checking account tiers (including free Kasasa Cash Checking and Prestige Club Checking), savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs). For lending, they offer personal loans, auto loans, home mortgage loans, home equity loans, personal credit cards, and a Ready Reserve Line of Credit. Business customers can access business checking, business savings, SBA loans, commercial real estate loans, equipment loans, and operation lines of credit.

Woodland Bank distinguishes itself through local market expertise, particularly in the Itasca County housing market, emphasizing quick processing and local support for mortgage lending. They feature Kasasa-branded reward accounts that provide cash back and savings bonuses based on customer activity. The bank offers modern conveniences including mobile banking with mobile deposit capabilities, online bill pay, business online cash management, merchant card services, and identity theft protection. Their website explicitly communicates anti-fraud practices, stating they will never call, text, or email requesting personal financial information.

Woodland Bank is a legitimate regional bank suitable for customers seeking traditional banking services with local branch access and personalized service. However, as a regional institution, they may not offer the interest rates or product breadth of larger national banks. Customers should compare their rates and fees against national competitors, as the website does not prominently display rate information in public areas, requiring customers to contact the bank directly at 218-327-4000 or visit physical locations. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts (Free Kasasa Cash, Kasasa Cash Back, Woodland Easy, Investors Choice, Prestige Club)
Personal savings and money market accounts
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and Health Savings Accounts (HSAs)
Home mortgage loans and home equity loans
Business checking and business savings accounts
SBA small business loans and commercial real estate loans
Equipment loans and operation lines of credit
Online bill pay and business online cash management
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government, protecting deposits up to applicable limits
Free Kasasa checking accounts with cash back rewards tied to account activity
Multiple loan options including SBA small business loans and commercial real estate financing
Mobile banking app with mobile deposit capability for on-the-go account management
Local mortgage expertise emphasizing quick processing and local support for Itasca County housing market
Dedicated business banking services including online cash management and merchant card services
Explicit fraud protection messaging and clear anti-phishing policy communicated to customers
cons: Limited geographic footprint as regional bank may restrict physical branch access for non-local customers
Interest rates and fee schedules not transparently displayed on website homepage, requiring customers to call or visit branches
Website lacks comparison tools or rate calculators for major products like mortgages and CDs in prominent locations
No mention of non-traditional products like investment services, wealth management, or advisory services
Limited information about APR ranges or lending criteria publicly available online

contact.address: 217 Main Ave E, Deer River, MN 56636

best_for: Residents of northern Minnesota (Itasca County area) seeking local banking with branch access
Small to medium-sized businesses needing comprehensive business banking and SBA loan options
Homebuyers in the local market looking for mortgage loans with personalized local support
Customers seeking reward-based checking accounts through Kasasa partnership programs
diagnosis: Woodland Bank is best suited for Minnesota residents and businesses in the Itasca County region seeking community-oriented banking with FDIC protection and local branch access. The main caveat is that as a regional bank, they may not compete on rates or product breadth with national banks, and customers outside their service area will lack convenient branch access, though digital banking options partially mitigate this limitation.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 217 Main Ave E, Deer River, MN 56636
